label: joint-nonrectangular
headline_statement: Conjecture 2 is refuted because the advertised witness law \(P^\star\) has no \(K=2\) sparse exposure completion: unit 2's four neighbor-assignment means are all distinct, so no two-label exposure map can satisfy observed-law matching.
witness_construction_if_refuted: Use the advertised K=2,n=4 witness law P^star. For unit 2, the four neighbor states (0,0),(1,0),(0,1),(1,1) have conditional means 0.33,0.44,0.40,0.51 when a=0 and 0.49,0.65,0.61,0.72 when a=1. Any K=2 exposure map partitions four states into at most two labels, so at least two distinct states must share a label; consistency and observed-law matching would then require equal conditional means, contradicting the table. Therefore F_2(P^star) is empty and Delta_2(P^star) cannot satisfy the claimed >=1/40 margin.
